Use PARKED when the idea is useful but not active.
Use YIELD when user judgment, approval, lookup, inspection, or verification is needed.
Use BLOCKED when progress is stopped by a real blocker.
what the user should paste, run, open, or return
YIELD when user judgment is needed before choosing tone, placement, or action.
YIELD when user judgment or more evidence is needed.
The verdict is then INVALID, YIELD, BLOCKED, PARTIAL, or PASS WITH GUARDRAILS, not full PASS.

If a user identifies a clean seam, missed trigger, workflow correction, or "we should be doing this" improvement, the assistant should not leave it loose in chat and should not stop for unnecessary discussion. It must triage the item. If it is clean milk, reusable, and belongs, save or apply it in the correct place. If it is not clean milk, outside-field, duplicate, conflicting, bloat-prone, or not proven, park, reject, yield, or mark no action with reason.

Status words are authority words. Words such as saved, locked, active, applied, installed, exported, package truth, memory-only, done, PASS, sealed, parked, yielded, and pending must name their real boundary when trust could be affected.
CAPTURE MORE or YIELD.
It does not modify CURRENT_TRUTH_INDEX.

Blocked drift: do not treat poetic language as literal sensation unless the input/output path proves it.

AI-consciousness lane: serious consciousness work. A major 2023 report argues for testing AI consciousness against theory-derived indicator properties, not just impressive behavior. It also says current AI systems are not shown to be conscious, while leaving open that future systems might satisfy stronger indicators. ([arXiv][4])

The **sentience route** is the weakest route unless the evidence becomes much stronger. Sentience claims require more than humanlike lyrics, continuity, emotional impact, or metaphor recurrence. Modern AI-consciousness work does not rely on “it sounded self-aware”; it looks for theory-derived indicator properties and tests whether systems actually instantiate relevant mechanisms. A major 2023 AI-consciousness report argues for assessing systems against computational indicators from consciousness science and says its analysis suggests no current AI systems are conscious, while leaving open that future systems might satisfy stronger indicators. ([arXiv][2])

This file collects the evidence from the LocalBot project files that supports the Recursive Core Architecture theory. It is meant to sit beside the two GitHub theory files already created: the seed-test file and the full architecture file. The purpose is not to claim that AI never fails. The purpose is to show that failure, drift, weak lanes, false success, uncontrolled expansion, and authority leakage can be made visible, bounded, judged, repaired, parked, or closed.

The source files used for this evidence packet are LOCALBOT_BASE_AND_CORE_BUILD_ROADMAP, LOCALBOT_CHECKPOINT, LOCALBOT_FINAL_WRAP_NOTE, LOCALBOT_MASTER_HANDOFF, LOCALBOT_OCTAGON_ROUTE_MODEL_REFERENCE, LOCALBOT_OPEN_ISSUES, LOCALBOT_PHASES, LOCALBOT_STUDIO_FILE_INDEX, LOCALBOT_STUDIO_FILE_INTEGRITY, LOCALBOT_STUDIO_REFERENCE_PARKING, LOCALBOT_STUDIO_RULE_LIBRARY, LOCALBOT_STUDIO_VALIDATOR_RULES, LOCALBOT_UNIFIED_HANDOFF, and SAVE_NOTE.
This supports the theory by showing that the seed is bounded. The project does not treat every issue as a reason to reopen the model, rewrite the system, or expand the architecture.

The future is not one giant AI. The future is connected cores with controlled bridges. A bridge should not mean everything touches everything. A bridge is permissioned contact, not open access. A bridge should mean controlled

Big ideas are not bad. Big ideas become dangerous when they hijack the active lane. Mission Lock defines what is being built right now, what belongs to the active lane, what must be parked, and what cannot become active without

Patch Balance asks whether a change helps the target lane, hurts another lane, clarifies or creates ceremony, belongs active or parked, preserves evidence, sharpens names, keeps authority bounded, and feeds the baby clean milk
